nodes.title: Ashes Beneath Silk
body: The Han court still stands, but authority has fractured into private armies and local granaries.
You decide to answer collapse with structure before rivals can define the north.

choices.label: Deploy Vanguard
choices.subtext: Secure initiative before the court can stall you.

nodes.title: Council of Methods
body: After two field actions, your officers demand direction: terror, doctrine or calculated patronage.
Your answer will determine where pressure will be applied to next

choices.label: Public Supression
choices.subtext: Break resistance openly to establish fear backed compliance.

choices.label: Doctrine Enforcement
choices.subtext: Reform command ranks and force discipline through execution orders.

choices.label: Co-opt the Militias
choices.subtext: Absorb local captains under your banner while they still fear exposure

nodes.title: Order by Fear
body: You order public punishments at road junctions and granary towns to make an example of dissenters.
Compliance rises quickly; resentment does too. You move before resentment can organize.

choices.label: Lead the Crackdown
choices.subtext: Demonstrate command directly in the field

nodes.title: Rule by Code
body: You restructure command with formal regulations, rotating officers before they form local factions
Discipline hardens, but every decree now requires enforcement under pressure.

nodes.title: Order by Absorption
body: You asorb militia leaders with rank, pay and surveillance. Their men now march under your supply rules.
The arrangement is useful but unstable. You must act before they can leverage their local influence against you.

choices.label: Press the Palace Line
choices.subtext: Convert fresh loyalties into operational pressure.

nodes.title: The Emperor's Shadow
body: With regional resistance crushed, the final question is political lineage:fguard the throne, control it, or bypass it.
Each path carries a different war doctrine into Act I.

choices.label: Protect the Emperor
choices.subtext: Claim authority through guardianship and formal legitimacy.

choices.label: Control the Emperor
choices.subtext: Use the throne as instrument of command and legal cover.

choices.label: Ignore the Throne
choices.subtext: Build extra-institutional authority through logistics and field coercion.

nodes.title: Chancellor's Leverage
body: You declare protection of the throne as state doctrine, forcing rivals to challenge you as rebels, not peers.

nodes.title: Chancellor's Leverage
body: You control petitions, appointments, and proclamations. Opposition must now fight both armies and paperwork.
